content:
✨ terminal | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide an advanced, self-hosted trading interface for Polymarket, enabling active traders to monitor markets, analyze trader performance, and execute trades with enhanced efficiency and control.
✨ Key Features: • Self-hosted Polymarket trading terminal
📖 Summary: This repository provides an open-source, self-hosted trading terminal for Polymarket, offering a Bloomberg-style dark UI with real-time order book data via SSE and self-custodial trading execution. It aims to enhance the user experience for active traders by providing advanced features like detailed trader leaderboards and keyboard navigation, all without third-party relayer fees or backend secrets.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🌟 blackcandy | Ruby
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide users with a personal, self-hosted music streaming service, offering control over their music library and playback experience.
✨ Key Features: • Self-hosted music streaming server • Web interface for music playback and management • Docker installation for ease of use • Support for PostgreSQL database • Mobile applications available for iOS and Android
📖 Summary: Black Candy is a self-hosted music streaming server that allows users to manage and stream their personal music library. It provides a web interface for playback and management, with optional mobile applications for access on the go.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🌟 Falcon-Perception | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: Performing object detection, instance segmentation, and OCR on images using natural language queries through an efficient inference engine.
✨ Key Features: • Natively multimodal Transformer model • Object detection, instance segmentation, and OCR capabilities • Inference engines for PyTorch (CUDA) and MLX (Apple Silicon) • Paged and batch inference options
📖 Summary: Falcon Perception is a PyTorch inference engine for a natively multimodal, autoregressive Transformer model capable of performing object detection, instance segmentation, and OCR from natural language queries. It offers various inference engines, including paged and batch processing for PyTorch and MLX (Apple Silicon), and supports deployment via an inference server.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

💡 cybermon | Svelte
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide users with a sleek, mobile-first dashboard for real-time monitoring of their computer's hardware performance, leveraging data from LibreHardwareMonitor.
✨ Key Features: • Futuristic mobile hardware monitoring dashboard • Integrates with LibreHardwareMonitor • Visualizes system statistics (CPU, GPU, RAM, etc.) • Remote web server for data fetching • Configurable device management
📖 Summary: CyberMon is a futuristic mobile hardware monitoring dashboard built with Svelte. It visualizes system statistics fetched from LibreHardwareMonitor, providing users with a clear overview of their device's performance on a mobile interface.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 Netflix-Prime-Auto-Skip | JavaScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: To automatically skip non-content segments like ads and intros on streaming platforms, providing a seamless viewing experience.
✨ Key Features: • Automatic skipping of ads, intros, recaps, and credits • Support for major streaming services including Netflix, Prime Video, Disney+, Crunchyroll, and HBO • Available as browser extensions for Chrome, Firefox, and Edge • Installable on Android via Firefox or Kiwi Browser
📖 Summary: This repository provides browser extensions for Chrome, Firefox, and Edge that automatically skip advertisements, intros, recaps, and credits on various major streaming services. It aims to enhance the viewing experience by removing unwanted content segments.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

✨ loopi | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: Automating repetitive tasks on a desktop computer, including web browsing, application interaction, system operations, and AI-driven workflows, all managed through a visual interface or CLI.
✨ Key Features: • Browser Automation • Desktop Control (mouse, keyboard) • System Commands (shell execution) • AI Agents (local and cloud LLMs) • 80+ API Integrations
📖 Summary: Loopi is an open-source desktop automation platform that allows users to build and run complex workflows locally. It offers visual workflow building, real browser automation, desktop control, system command execution, AI agent integration, and over 80 API integrations.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source
